description: Augment trunk coordinates with r^{-1/2} singular basis terms (1/sqrt(r), sin/cos theta/2) to give the network an explicit representation of crack-tip stress singularity
apply_when: near-tip error high, ensemble_std large near tip, stress_intensity or near_tip loss terms not converging

description: Append Heaviside sign function H(x) as a trunk feature so the network can represent displacement discontinuity across crack faces and satisfy traction-free BC
apply_when: bc_weight loss not decreasing, traction-free BC violations, displacement shows no opening discontinuity

description: Concentrate PINO collocation points within a small disk around the crack tip (60-80%) rather than uniform placement, so physics loss targets the high-residual singularity zone
apply_when: PINO equilibrium loss converging slowly, physics loss < 0.5% of data loss, near-tip error persists after Williams features

description: Predict phase-field damage variable d alongside displacement (output_dim=3) to handle crack propagation datasets where crack tip position varies between samples
apply_when: dataset has varying crack tip positions, J-integral loss not converging, surrogate fails to generalize across crack configurations

description: Add energy-release-rate consistency loss (J_pred vs J_ref) after Williams and K_I terms are stable, to improve fracture parameter accuracy for life-prediction use
apply_when: K_I prediction error > 5%, j_weight inactive, stress_intensity and near_tip terms already stable and active

description: Decompose trunk features into symmetric (u_y) and antisymmetric (u_x) components using Heaviside, exploiting crack-plane symmetry to halve effective learning difficulty
apply_when: symmetric geometry, far-field swirling artifacts despite sin/cos trunk features, Mode II contribution < 5%
